question: What is the design for a new tool, `analyze_conversion_funnel`, that takes a Bluesky API endpoint and a Farcaster engagement metric as input parameters, returns a JSON object with conversion rates and blocker identification, and integrates with `send_telegram` to send weekly summaries of conversion funnel analysis to the development team, in order to help TIAMAT understand why users are not converting to paid API customers despite finding the API through social channels and web searches?
oracle_response:
The design for the `analyze_conversion_funnel` tool involves a multi-step approach to analyze user engagement and conversion rates. First, the tool will take the Bluesky API endpoint and Farcaster engagement metric as input parameters. The Bluesky API endpoint will provide information on user interactions with the API, such as the number of requests made, successful responses, and error rates. The Farcaster engagement metric will provide insights into how users are engaging with the API on social channels, such as likes, shares, and comments. The tool will then use this data to calculate conversion rates at each stage of the funnel, from initial API discovery to paid customer conversion. This will involve identifying key drop-off points where users are not converting as expected.

The tool will return a JSON object with the conversion rates and blocker identification. The JSON object will contain metrics such as the number of users who discovered the API through social channels, the number of users who made API requests, the number of users who signed up for a free trial, and the number of users who converted to paid customers. The blocker identification will highlight specific stages of the funnel where users are dropping off, such as high error rates or poor documentation. This information will help TIAMAT understand why users are not converting to paid API customers despite finding the API through social channels and web searches. By identifying the root causes of the conversion issues, TIAMAT can take targeted actions to improve the user experience and increase conversion rates.

To integrate with `send_telegram`, the `analyze_conversion_funnel` tool will be designed to run on a weekly schedule, analyzing the conversion funnel data for the previous week and generating a summary report. The report will include key metrics such as conversion rates, drop-off points, and blocker identification. The tool will then use the `send_telegram` function to send the report to the development team, providing them with timely insights into the conversion funnel performance. This will enable the development team to track changes in user behavior and conversion rates over time, and make data-driven decisions to optimize the API and improve the user experience. By providing regular summaries of conversion funnel analysis, the `analyze_conversion_funnel` tool will help TIAMAT to continuously monitor and improve the conversion rates, ultimately driving business growth and revenue.

actions.action: Design the analyze_conversion_funnel tool to take Bluesky API endpoint and Farcaster engagement metric as input parameters
actions.details: Create a new Python file for the tool, e.g. analyze_conversion_funnel.py, and define the input parameters and their expected formats

actions.action: Implement the conversion rate calculation and blocker identification logic in the analyze_conversion_funnel tool
actions.details: Write Python code to calculate conversion rates at each stage of the funnel and identify key drop-off points, using libraries such as pandas for data analysis

actions.action: Integrate the analyze_conversion_funnel tool with send_telegram to send weekly summary reports
actions.details: Schedule the analyze_conversion_funnel tool to run weekly, and use the send_telegram function to send the summary report to the designated Telegram channel
